event.description:
Add a median(values) function to src/stats.js, exported alongside sum and mean, and cover it with tests in test/stats.test.js.
criterion 1: median is exported from src/stats.js and returns the middle value for odd-length arrays
criterion 2: npm test passes including the new median tests

event.text:
The prior turn's work was already complete and matches the new human directive — no repeat work needed. I verified the current state:

- `median()` in `src/stats.js` throws `TypeError` on `[]`, returns the average of the two middle values for even-length arrays, and the middle value for odd-length arrays.
- `test/stats.test.js` has assertions for odd-length (`median([3,1,2]) === 2`), even-length (`median([1,2,3,4]) === 2.5`), single-element, and the empty-array throw.
- `npm test` passes, 3/3 tests green.

Both the original goal and the human directive's added requirements (even-length averaging, empty-array throw, both covered by tests) are already satisfied by the existing diff — nothing further to change.

```pitwall
{"status": "done", "summary": "Verified after an interrupted-turn recovery that median(values) was already correctly implemented in src/stats.js (exported alongside sum/mean, throws TypeError on empty input, averages the two middle values for even-length arrays, returns the middle value for odd-length arrays) and already covered by tests in test/stats.test.js for odd-length, even-length, single-element, and empty-array cases. Re-ran `npm test` to confirm all 3 tests pass with no regressions. No code changes were needed this turn since the human directive's requirements were already satisfied by the prior work."}
```
